title: TUI format_payload ASCII check may misclassify control characters
description:
File: python/src/lichen/tui/app.py
Lines: 62-78

The format_payload function checks for printable ASCII:

    text = data.decode("ascii")
    if all(32 <= ord(c) < 127 for c in text):
        return f"{hex_str} ({text})"

This excludes DEL (127) but includes all chars 32-126. However:
- Tab (9), newline (10), carriage return (13) are excluded even though they're common in text
- Some extended ASCII chars might be displayable depending on terminal

This is mostly cosmetic but could cause confusion when viewing packets that contain tabs or newlines - they'll show as pure hex when users might expect to see the text content.

Impact: Minor UX issue - legitimate text payloads with whitespace show as hex-only.

Fix: Consider including common whitespace characters in the printable check, or use a more standard definition like str.isprintable().
notes: Fixed by updating format_payload to use UTF-8 + isprintable() or whitespace controls (mirrors kiss/payload_fmt.py logic). Commit: 81bc208e7
close_reason: Implemented improved printable check using isprintable() + common whitespace; tested with various payloads including newlines/tabs/UTF8; tests and ruff pass. Commit 81bc208e7
